Which of the following rivers flows from south to north?
questions.explanation: <p>The Betwa or Betravati is a tributary of the Yamuna. It rises in the Vindhya Range just north of Hoshangabad in Madhya Pradesh and flows north-east through Madhya Pradesh and Orchha to Uttar Pradesh. Other rivers that flow from South to North are Narmada, Chambal, Son and Tugabhadra.</p>

The river Beas originates in the Pir Panjal range near which pass?
questions.explanation:
The Correct answer is Rohtang Pass.

Key Points

  • The Beas River originates from the Beas Kund, located near the Rohtang Pass, in the Pir Panjal range of the Himalayas.
  • The Rohtang Pass is situated in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India.
  • The Beas River is an important river in northern India and flows through the states of Himachal Pradesh and Punjab.
  • The river has a total length of approximately 470 kilometers and merges with the Sutlej River in Punjab.
  • The name "Beas" is derived from the Sanskrit word "Vipasha."
  • Rohtang Pass is a high mountain pass located at an altitude of approximately 3,978 meters (13,050 feet) above sea level.
  • This pass is a gateway to the Lahaul and Spiti valleys and is a popular tourist destination known for its breathtaking views and adventure activities.

Additional Information

  • Shipki La
    • Shipki La is a high-altitude mountain pass located in the Himachal Pradesh state of India.
    • This pass connects India with Tibet and is used for trade and transportation.
    • The Sutlej River, another important river of India, enters India from Tibet near the Shipki La pass.
  • Parangla
    • Parangla Pass is a mountain pass located in the state of Himachal Pradesh.
    • It is a trekking route that connects the Spiti Valley in Himachal Pradesh with the Ladakh region.
    • This pass is known for its scenic beauty and remote location.
  • Baralacha La
    • Baralacha La is another high mountain pass in the Himalayas, situated in the state of Himachal Pradesh.
    • This pass lies on the Leh-Manali Highway and is situated at an altitude of 4,890 meters (16,040 feet).
    • It is the source of the Chandra and Bhaga rivers, which later join to form the Chenab River.

questions.statement: The National Development Council was established in accordance with:
What is the height of the highest peak "Shilla" in the Zaskar Mountain Range?
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is 7,026 metres.

Key Points

  • The Zaskar Mountain Range is a part of the Trans-Himalayan system located in the northern part of India.
  • Shilla Peak, with an elevation of 7,026 metres, is the highest peak in this range.
  • It is situated in the Kinnaur district of Himachal Pradesh, India.
  • The Zaskar Range is known for its rugged terrain, high-altitude passes, and extreme climatic conditions.
  • This region is a popular destination for mountaineering and trekking enthusiasts due to its challenging routes and scenic beauty.
  • The Zaskar River, an important tributary of the Indus River, flows through the region, contributing to its geographical significance.
  • The area around Shilla Peak is rich in biodiversity, with unique flora and fauna adapted to its high-altitude environment.

Additional Information

  • 6,026 metres
    • This elevation does not correspond to the highest peaks of the Zaskar Range but may refer to other peaks or geographical locations in the region.
  • 7,025 metres
    • This elevation is close to the correct height but is not accurate for Shilla Peak.
  • 6,500 metres
    • This elevation is significantly lower than Shilla Peak and could correspond to other peaks in the vicinity of the Zaskar Range.
